data.title: NomosLogic Founder Matt Hardy Launches Lyceum and Odyssey on Dendrite Lite
data.description: Salt Lake City-based molecular medicine infrastructure company, NomosLogic, Inc., has introduced two new consumer offerings on Dendrite Lite, accessible at lite.dendrite.com. Lyceum is a personalized genetic literacy quiz created from an individual's unique biology. Odyssey encapsulates a user's genetic journey in seven chapters, explained in simple terms for all family members. These features are now live on Theogony, the latest interactive component in Dendrite Lite. This initiative marks a significant step for the company.

data.title: a2 Platinum USA Label Infant Formula Recalled Because of Possible Health Risk
data.description: A recall has been initiated by the a2 Milk Company for three batches of a2 Platinum Premium USA label infant formula 0-12 months due to the presence of cereulide, a heat-stable toxin produced by certain Bacillus cereus strains. The recall only affects products sold in the United States. Illness can result from consuming food contaminated with the toxin, and using hot water to prepare formula does not remove it.

data.title: CISA warns of critical Linux Copy Fail vulnerability permitting root access via 10 lines of Python.
data.description: A cybersecurity researcher has revealed that malicious parties can potentially obtain root access on Linux systems by utilizing just 10 lines of Python code that grants code execution capabilities.

data.title: Spirit Airlines cancels all flights, leaving passengers stranded. Traveler vows never to fly again, vowing to avoid them regardless.
data.description: Passengers were left stranded after the low-cost airline unexpectedly halted flights on Saturday. Many travelers had to quickly find alternate arrangements. A disappointed passenger remarked, "Even if they resume operations, I will not fly with them again."

data.title: Grandma's $1000 College Fund Gift Turned Him Into A Bitcoin Millionaire By Age 18: Here's How This High School Dropout Made It Happen
data.description: Entrepreneur Erik Finman made a pledge with his parents that he wouldn't have to attend college if he became a millionaire by the age of 18. He reached this goal through Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC). When Finman was only 12 years old, his grandmother gifted him $1,000 for his college fund, but he decided to invest it in Bitcoin in 2011 when it was valued at around $10, accumulating close to 100 BTC. By 2017, Bitcoin's value had surged to $16,000, making Finman a millionaire at 18. This success was a result of his persistence in buying and expanding his investments, along with his efforts to generate income as a teenager. The complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Parent company Payward, of Kraken, seals Bitnomial deal, enhancing presence in US crypto derivatives. Fully licensed, under CFTC, offers trading, clearing, and brokerage.
data.description: The recent acquisition has given access to a comprehensive derivatives stack that is fully licensed and falls under the oversight of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). This includes trading, clearing, and brokerage services.
